title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in governance rather than technical safety: OpenAI published a frontier-AI governance blueprint alongside broader policy positions on regulation, youth protections, and political advocacy. The most material update is the call for a federal framework for frontier-model safety, resilience, and national-security oversight, with adjacent proposals on standards and safeguards.

items.title: OpenAI proposes federal governance blueprint for frontier AI
items.why_it_matters: It pushes concrete U.S. oversight ideas for frontier-model safety, resilience, and national-security risk management.

items.title: OpenAI sets out public policy agenda on AI safety and standards
items.why_it_matters: It signals the company’s preferred regulatory approach on safety, youth protections, labor transition, and global standards.

items.title: OpenAI calls for global action on youth AI safety
items.why_it_matters: It frames youth safeguards as a governance priority and proposes international coordination on standards and protections.

items.title: OpenAI outlines its stance on AI policy and political advocacy
items.why_it_matters: It clarifies how the company presents its regulatory positions and safety advocacy to policymakers.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
